entitySuffixRule: Must contain the words "Limited Liability Company" or "Limited Company," or the abbreviations "LLC," "L.L.C.," "LC," "L.C.," or "Ltd. Liability Company."
distinguishabilityNote: Must be distinguishable upon the records of the Secretary of State as provided in W.S. 17-16-401. A name is distinguishable if it has one or more different letters or numerals, or a different sequence of letters or numerals, from other names on record. Differences that do not make a name distinguishable include entity-type words or abbreviations (corporation, company, incorporated, limited, L.P., LLC, and the like), the words "the," "and," or "a," and punctuation or special characters. A conflicting name may be used with the other holder's consent or under a certified court judgment.
nameReservationCostBasis: The Secretary of State fee to reserve a name is $60; a reservation is not required to file Articles of Organization.
notes: Wyoming's LLC Act (W.S. Title 17, ch. 29) defines "distinguishable" by statute through the Business Corporation Act (§ 17-16-401): entity-type words, the articles "the/and/a," and punctuation are ignored, so a real letter or number difference is required to clear a conflict. There is no banned-word list. Wyoming is a formation-haven state, with strong owner privacy and no member disclosure on the public record. The LLC filing fee is $100 and an annual report license tax ($60 minimum) is due the first day of the anniversary month. A reservation ($60, 120 days) is optional.
